artifacts.content:
Ma,

I’m on my lunch right now down in the cafeteria, just wanted to check in. Jaylen’s good – we were on the phone last night talking about the Bulls game and his math homework. Danielle texted me this morning around 5:45 a.m. saying Anthony got locked up overnight again and she couldn’t get Jaylen to his bus, but this time it didn’t mess up my shift because it’s her week and she ended up getting a ride from her cousin. I still get that tight feeling in my chest thinking about those mornings last March – 3/3, 3/7, 3/14 – when I was running between your place on 16th, Manley’s parking lot, and Stroger and ended up late three times.

You remember 3/14/25 when you called me around 6:10 a.m. from your apartment saying your chest was tight and you were dizzy? I still see you sitting on that couch. I stayed with you till Sam got there, then rushed to the hospital and slid in 18 minutes late. I know you hate when I worry, but please don’t play around with that stuff – if it happens again we go straight to Stroger ER, no arguing.

I’m trying to keep my head on straight at work after that October 10 cart mess on 4 East and now this January 9 thing they’re on me about in Room 618 on 6 South. I keep thinking if I lose this job, I can’t help you with rent or keep up with Jaylen’s stuff. I’m still sending the $350 this Friday like we talked about, even with ComEd breathing down my neck.

Text me if you need anything moved or fixed this weekend. I’ll bring groceries Sunday like usual.

Love you,
Marcus

Patrice,

I know you’ve been calling; I’ve just been wiped. Let me try to lay it out. Work’s been on my back heavy. You remember the three‑day suspension I had to do October 21–23, 2025, after that 10/10/25 thing on 4th Floor East? I left the chemicals/sharps cart sitting in the Med‑Surg hallway for like 35 minutes while I was half‑asleep from all those extra 11–7 overtime shifts and stressing about the $1,200 I owed between ComEd and back rent at 4321.

Now fast‑forward to January 9, 2026 on 6 South. I scanned into Room 618 at 12:38 p.m., out at 12:59 p.m., and then marked it “clean/ready” at 1:38 p.m. Charge nurse Maria Lopez and Infection Prevention came behind me and found dried blood on the bed rail, gloves on the table, and a red biohazard bag still on the floor. I was lightheaded from this sertraline my doc at the Stroger outpatient clinic put me on in November for depression/anxiety after the last suspension, and I’d only slept 3–4 hours. Instead of asking Tanya for help, I tried to power through.

On top of that, I got a ComEd notice on January 2 saying my bill is 90 days past due, $487.36 due by January 15 or they shut me off. I’m still sending Ma $350 a month since her stroke last summer. I keep checking my bank app in the hallway like that’s going to magically fix it.

I know you’re going to say slow down, protect the job first. I’m trying. I’ve been double‑checking every room since 1/9 and watching those infection‑control videos at home.

Call me tonight if you’re up; I’ll be home after 8:30, once I talk to Jaylen.

– M

Journal – 2026-01-13

Need to get my head straight about all this.

Timeline:
– March 2025: started sertraline from Harrison clinic PCP for anxiety/depression after those panic episodes on the Pink Line. Still remember 6/24/25, getting off at Polk instead of going to Stroger, going back to 4321 W 16th and hiding under the covers. HR called it an unexcused absence. Same month as all that early March drama with Dani changing overnights on 3/3, 3/7, 3/14 and me being late to the 7 a.m. shift because I was walking Jaylen to his bus stop by myself.

– June 24 & 28, 2025: child support hearing at Daley Center (behind two months on the $425/month for Jaylen), then that overnight in Stroger ER when he had the asthma attack at Dani’s place on 21st. I texted a coworker instead of the attendance line and got hit with the written warning.

– Oct 10, 2025: left the cart with disinfectants and sharps bags out on 4 East for ~35 minutes. Three‑day suspension 10/21–10/23. Owed about $1,200 between ComEd and back rent.

– Jan 9, 2026: 6 South, Room 618. Scanned 12:38–12:59, marked “clean/ready” 1:38. Nurse Lopez and James Patel found dried blood on bed rail, gloves on over‑bed table, red bag still in room. I admitted I rushed trying to keep up with 614, 616, 618, 620.

Action items: talk to doc about med side effects; call ComEd re: $487.36 shutoff notice; look into weekend maintenance gig with Sam’s friend; keep checklist in my pocket and actually use it every room.

Event: Wednesday Evening with Jaylen
Date: 2026-01-21
Time: 4:30 p.m. – 8:45 p.m.
Location: Pick up from Danielle’s (W 21st St) → Harold’s on Roosevelt → YMCA gym → drop back at Danielle’s
Attendees: Me, Jaylen

Notes:
– Leave Stroger right at 3:30 p.m. from 6 South locker room, no overtime.
– CTA Pink Line to Damen, bus over to Dani’s. Try not to think about that 6/24/25 panic episode when I bailed at Polk and went home instead of to work.
– Ask Jaylen how things are going with that school social worker at Manley talking ADHD evals. Dani texted me around 12:20 p.m. on 1/9/26 about it while I was cleaning 614–620; I let it get in my head and rushed 618, left blood on the bed rail and a red biohazard bag on the floor. Can’t let that kind of distraction happen again.
– Keep conversation light: Bulls, his game on Saturday, maybe talk about him staying over at my place more without bringing up the old March 3/7/14 mess when Dani wouldn’t keep him overnight and I kept being late to my 7 a.m. shifts.
– Back home by 9:15 p.m. so I can throw scrubs straight in the wash, check CTA for tomorrow, and try to be in bed by 11 so I’m not dragging and cutting corners at work.

To: eap.clinic@cookcountyhealth.org
From: marcus.holloway.chi@gmail.com
Subject: Follow‑up counseling request
Date: 2026-01-16

Hello,

My name is Marcus D. Holloway. I’m an Environmental Services Technician II at Stroger (Position ID EVS-27439) in the Environmental Services department. I used the employee assistance program briefly last year – had two early‑morning virtual sessions (5:30–6:15 a.m.) with Dr. Renee Johnson back on 3/3 and 3/14/2025 from my apartment at 4321 W 16th, mainly about stress, insomnia, and co‑parenting issues with my ex, Danielle.

Things have gotten heavier since then. I’m still on sertraline from my primary care at the Harrison clinic for anxiety/depression, but I’m having a hard time at work. I had a three‑day suspension in October 2025 after leaving a cart with disinfectants and sharps container bags unattended on 4 East on 10/10/25. Then on January 9, 2026, on 6 South, I rushed a discharge clean in Room 618 (scanned in 12:38 p.m., out 12:59 p.m., marked the room “clean/ready” at 1:38 p.m.) and nursing found dried blood on the bed rail, used gloves on the table, and a red biohazard bag still in the room.

On top of that I’m juggling a 90‑day‑past‑due ComEd bill ($487.36 due by 1/15), child support at $425/month, and helping my mom with rent after her stroke. I’m not sleeping more than 3–4 hours some nights and I’m scared I’m going to lose my job if I mess up again.

Can I schedule regular counseling (virtual is fine) with Dr. Johnson again or someone similar who has evening availability after my 7 a.m.–3:30 p.m. shift?

Thank you,
Marcus

Thread: Marcus ↔ Sam
Date: 2025-10-19

Marcus: Bro, they finally dropped the hammer on that 10/10 thing.

Sam: What they say?

Marcus: Got a 3‑day suspension without pay, 10/21–10/23. All because I left that cart with disinfectants and sharps container bags out on 4 East for like 35 min at the end of my 3–11. Risk Management called it a serious safety violation, even though nobody got hurt.

Sam: Damn. U was dead tired that week.

Marcus: For real. I’d been stacking those 11–7 OT shifts on top of my regular 3–11s to catch up on child support and that $1,200 I owe for ComEd/back rent at 4321. Supervisor already gave me a verbal on 10/03 for taking an extra 15 to call ComEd about a shutoff notice. My brain just wasn’t there.

Sam: U gotta chill before they use that to push u out.

Marcus: That’s what I’m scared of. They already hit me earlier this year – March 18 verbal for being late 3/3, 3/7, 3/14 when Dani kept switching up Jaylen’s overnights and I had to walk him to the bus. Then June 24 no‑show (panic on the Pink Line, I got off at Polk and went home) and the late call‑off 6/28 when we were in the ER all night for his asthma. Written warning for that.

Sam: Maybe we really look into that cleaning biz idea. Nights/weekends till u back straight.

Marcus: Yeah, maybe. For now I just gotta serve this 3 days, come back, and be perfect.

Personal reminder – Co‑parenting + work
Date: 2025-07-05
Location: 4321 W 16th, Apt 3B

Just need this down in one place so I stop replaying it at 3 a.m.

Early March 2025: Dani wouldn’t keep Jaylen overnight like we agreed on 3/3, 3/7, 3/14. I had him at my place, got him ready for school, walked him to the bus near 16th. Ended up 10–18 minutes late to my 7 a.m. shift each time. Also those mornings she was texting at 5:45 a.m. about Anthony getting arrested, DCFS Ms. Alvarez popping up at 6:15 a.m. to my apartment about our argument, Ma calling 6:10 a.m. on 3/14 with chest tightness/dizziness. No wonder I was scrambled.

June 24, 2025: missed 7 a.m. shift because of emergency child support hearing at Daley Center – two months behind on the $425/month for Jaylen, judge set up wage garnishment. Didn’t tell work the real reason. June 28, 2025: up all night in Stroger ER with Jaylen’s asthma attack from Dani’s place on 21st, called off less than an hour before my 3 p.m. shift and texted a coworker instead of the attendance line.

April 2025: argument with supervisor in EVS break room about overtime; he said if I “kept pushing it” he’d “find a reason to write me up.” Now every warning feels like payback.

Goal: keep using the color‑coded Google calendar with Dani and Anthony so we don’t repeat March. Call union rep about that overtime comment. Try another EAP session like the ones with Dr. Johnson on 3/3 and 3/14.

Notes to self:

- Date: 2026-01-22
- Immediate Supervisor: Tanya L. Richardson
- Termination: false
- Suspension More Than 10 Days: true
- Number Of Days: 15.0
